title: puccinellia decumbens
intro: Puccinellia decumbens, also known as bent grass, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is often found in wet meadows and along streams. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Puccinellia decumbens include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, and it can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or salve for topical application.
